plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the single-name concentration mandate and reduce broad market exposure slightly.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, aligning with capital preservation.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and stability.
Buy XLV at 10% to diversify into a high-quality, lower-volatility sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a target of ~1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely and trim SPAL to 3% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure while keeping a small allocation.
Add to CRWD, pressing a winning tech name with strong momentum.
Buy LRCX at 7% to add a high-scoring semiconductor setup, diversifying within tech.
Maintain a cash buffer of around 10% to manage risk.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index exposure with quality tech, accepting moderate drawdown potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit a losing position and reallocate capital into stronger setups.
Trim SMH to 8% to reduce exposure to a downtrending sector.
Buy LRCX at 12% and ASML at 10%, leaning into high-scoring semiconductor setups.
Increase SPAL to 8%, utilizing leveraged ETFs for aggressive growth potential.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer, accepting higher risk for increased upside.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% per month, aiming for stretch growth by using leverage and concentrating in high-scoring setups. Be aware that this approach significantly increases both upside potential and drawdown risk, and leveraged ETFs are subject to decay and rebalancing.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad index exposure and meet the concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure, prioritizing stability.
Trim CRWD and SMH to 5% each, reducing high-beta single names and sectors.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize allocation to income-generating ETFs.
Maintain a cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliable income.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield through income ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions, but growth potential is minimal and NAV can still fall in a selloff.
